scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Stocking of fingerlings in reservoir - Gujarat
page_title: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Stocking of fingerlings in reservoir - Gujarat

details: The Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland) with the component of the Stocking of fingerlings in reservoir (1000 fingerlings/ha) (3.0 lakh / 1 lakh fingerlings, introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, Through this scheme, various forms of assistance are provided for inland fisheries activities to individuals, societies, self-help groups, and other private entities. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% from the Government of Gujarat.
Increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and provide employment to people involved in inland fisheries activities.
Assistance is provided for accelerating inland fish farming activities in freshwater bodies, increasing inland fish production and boosting inland fisheries.

Except for water bodies or ponds, in less land, less water, Biofloc with source as well, Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS). By creating fish tanks with this method, more fish production can be obtained in less land and less water. If more beneficiaries get the benefit of the subsidy of this scheme, more fish production can be achieved with less water sources and less land.
Inland fish production capacity can be increased by the establishment of freshwater hatcheries.
benefits: Stocking of fingerlings in reservoirs: Assistance is provided for stocking 1,000 fingerlings per hectare. The unit cost of the fingerling is ₹3.00/- per piece.
Assistance of 40% of the unit cost is given for the General category.
Assistance of 60% of the unit cost is given for Women, Scheduled Caste, and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries.
The assistance is paid through a 60% contribution from the Government of India and a 40% contribution from the State Government.
The benefit of this component can be availed once in a lifetime.
eligibility: The applicant must be an individual, society, self-help group, association, reservoir lease holder, or private entity involved in inland fisheries activities.
The applicant must be above 18 years of age.
The applicant must have their own bank account.

The applicant must have their own private land or a minimum 10-year lease agreement for land to avail construction-related assistance on land. In case of co-owners, the consent form of co-owners must be submitted.
The applicant must have a Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhaar Card/Passport as proof of identity.
All categories (General, Women, Scheduled Caste, and Scheduled Tribe) will receive benefits under the Inland Schemes.
Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries are required to have a Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe certificate to avail benefits under the inland components.

online: The applicant may visit and apply online at the
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side, then click on "Beneficiary Registration" and select any one from the 'Farmer' or 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type.

Enter the 'Company Name' and Address if you have selected 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type. And for all select the District, Taluka, and Village, choose 'what type of account holder you are?', enter your Account Number and Name, solve the Captcha, and click 'Save and Next'.
Enter your Mobile Number and E-mail address, click 'Send OTP', enter the received OTP, create and confirm a Password, solve the Captcha, and click 'Submit'.
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side of the homepage. On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Agricultural Schemes’.
Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button, and an application page will open.
Click on the "Apply New" button and submit a new application.
Click on the "Update Application" button to add corrections to the application.
Once the application is done, confirm it.
Take a printout of the confirmed application.

documents_required: Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhaar Card/Passport (as proof of identity).
Land Ownership Documents (if applicant has own private land).
Consent Form of Co-owners (in case of co-owned land).
Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe Certificate (for Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries).

faqs.question: Who all are eligible to apply for the benefits under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Individuals, societies, self-help groups, associations, reservoir lease holders, and private entities involved in inland fisheries activities can take advantage of the components of this scheme.

faqs.question: What is the minimum age required to be eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: An applicant must be above 18 years of age to be eligible to apply for the benefits.

faqs.question: Do I need to own the land to receive construction-related assistance?
faqs.answer: To avail of assistance for construction, you must have either your own private land or a land lease agreement for a minimum of 10 years.

faqs.question: What if the land I want to use for the project is co-owned by others?
faqs.answer: If the land is co-owned, you must submit a consent form from all the co-owners to be eligible for the assistance.

faqs.question: How much assistance will I get for stocking fingerlings in a reservoir?
faqs.answer: For stocking fingerlings, you will receive assistance of 40% of the unit cost if you are in the general category, and 60% if you are a woman, or belong to the SC or ST category.

faqs.question: How is the assistance amount for stocking fingerlings distributed between the government and me?
faqs.answer: The assistance is paid through a 60% contribution from the Government of India and a 40% contribution from the State Government.

faqs.question: What is the maximum number of fingerlings that can be stocked per hectare under this component?
faqs.answer: The standard of assistance for stocking fingerlings in reservoirs is 1,000 fingerlings per hectare.

faqs.question: How does this scheme help in increasing fish production?
faqs.answer: The scheme increases fish production by supporting activities like rearing fish in reservoirs, accelerating inland fish farming in freshwater bodies, and establishing freshwater hatcheries.

faqs.question: What documents are accepted as proof of identity for the application?
faqs.answer: You can use a Voter ID, Ration Card, Aadhar Card, or Passport as a valid proof of identity for the application.
